items.text: A useful research thread about turning scattered bookmarks into a public shelf with context, curation, and links back to source material.
New tool demo: local-first bookmark search with theme clustering, markdown exports, and a clean static site output.
Analysis: private archives become more useful when selected trails are made public as readable shelves instead of raw link dumps.
Strategy note: a curator site should combine public shelves, a portfolio, and private research loops without leaking raw personal data.
